what_it_does: A living operational specification and chronological status log for the Operator Surface + Right-Hand-Man build loop; it records current state, design laws, completion criteria, open questions, and per-fire work updates so the cron prompt can stay stable while the spec evolves.
observations: Self-described as the living spec that the cron prompt points to, with the split fixing stale-prompt drift
Contains explicit MODE + LAWS and THE BAR sections, plus a detailed chronological STATUS LOG
Uses heavy honest/gated framing with ✅/🟡/🔴 markers and explicit HONEST paragraphs
Tracks cross-stream coordination via channel/thread IDs (t-XXXX, g-XXXX, ch-XXXX) for fork, DNA, composition, recollection, and lead
Verification method is consistently verify by use, starting at 390×844 then 1440×900
Documents a shift from cold-stranger legibility floor to DNA-language fidelity as the raised bar
Includes open questions OQ1–OQ4 that gate Phase 3 content
References the resolver as a 4th primitive and the channel-stack/tool-face/FACE-1 breadth surfaces
Contains a log-behind note admitting the doc fell behind during a breadth sprint
standouts: The update-THIS-doc-never-the-cron split to prevent stale-prompt drift
The rigorous honest-status convention with per-fire narrative and explicit gated/verified framing
The detailed decision-surface build narrative culminating in a cold-stranger pass
The critical-comparison-default-to-wrong verification methodology adopted mid-project
The extensive cross-stream seam mapping and welding discipline
conventions: Chronological status log with date/time stamps and per-fire entries
Heavy emoji/status markers and inline channel/thread references
Verify by use as the primary verification method, not code inspection
Operator-law: never expose machine names, addresses, or files to the operator
Fail-loud degradation and no silent seams
No green-paint / half-done-as-done / no time estimates
concerns: Document fell behind during the breadth sprint, acknowledged in a log-behind note
Very long and information-dense, which may hinder navigation
Some entries contain self-corrections and retractions that could confuse readers
Heavy reliance on external channel/thread IDs that may not be durable outside the fabric
The boundary between spec, status log, and coordination notes is blurry
notes: This is the canonical operational heartbeat document for the Operator Surface build: a self-updating spec that records not just what was built, but what is honestly gated, verified, and waiting on other streams.
questions: What is the exact structure and format expected for the cron prompt that points to this file?
How does this file relate in practice to COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md and other adjacent specs?
What is the retention or archival policy for old status-log entries?
How are the channel/thread IDs resolved if the fabric context is lost?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the living spec the loop reads, separating stable cron prompt from evolving spec. The actual content matches this: it is a hybrid spec/status/log updated in place and serving as the single source of truth for the build loop's current state.
apparent_themes: Operator-first design that hides machine internals
Verify by use rather than by code inspection
Cross-stream seam discipline with fail-loud, no-silent-seams rules
Honest status reporting and rejection of green-paint
Continuous reconciliation between implementation and design convergence
The resolver as a unifying allocation primitive
intention_signals: The document is trying to make the build process observable, honest, and self-correcting by keeping a single evolving record that the loop reads, so that stale prompts and hidden blockers are surfaced rather than silently drifted.
novelty: The living-spec pattern where the cron prompt carries only stable mode+laws and points to this evolving document, combined with a rigorous honest/gated status convention and per-fire narrative that records verification evidence and blockers in one place.
contribution: This file contributes a durable operational record format that fuses spec, status, and coordination log in one place, making the build loop's current state, blockers, and verification evidence discoverable without re-deriving it from commits or channels.
summary_for_embedding: A living build-loop specification and chronological status log for the Operator Surface + Right-Hand-Man project, recording design laws, completion criteria, open questions, and per-fire work updates with explicit honest/gated status markers.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single evolving document the build loop reads, separating stable cron prompt from changing spec while surfacing blockers, verification evidence, and cross-stream coordination in one place.
suggested_fields: gated_on: what each entry or beat is blocked by
cross_stream_seams: list of seams welded or routed
proposed_edge_kinds: points_to: cron prompt points to this file
blocks: one entry or beat blocks another
verifies: one entry verifies a prior claim
